documents.title: THE SPECTRAL CAUL
documents.subtitle: Equipment Profile — Class III Psi-Shielding — Mark IV Revision

sections.heading: DESCRIPTION
sections.body: The Spectral Caul is a close-fitting mesh hood with 12 neural contact points distributed across the crown, temples, and base of the skull. It is worn in direct contact with the scalp. Its function is to dampen and filter void emanations before they reach the wearer's cognitive systems.

sections.heading: EFFICACY
sections.body:
Against Class I-II void emanations: highly effective. Extended duration possible with standard monitoring.

Against Class III emanations: effective with significant attenuation. Operatives report 'pressure' behind the eyes and occasional mild aphasia at extended durations.

Against Class IV emanations: provides approximately 8-15 additional minutes of cognitive coherence before standard exposure progression. It does not protect you from the Pallid Watcher. It only slows the process.

This limitation is documented internally. Operatives deploying to Class IV encounter zones should be informed. Whether they are informed is at the discretion of operational command.

sections.heading: DEVELOPMENT
sections.body:
The Spectral Caul Mark I was developed in Cycles 4418-4419, following the Omicron Collapse, using neural tissue samples from void-exposed casualties. This is not disclosed in standard equipment briefings.

The Mark IV revision has improved efficacy by approximately 23% over the Mark I. The development methodology has not changed.

sections.heading: AVAILABILITY
sections.body:
34 units currently active. Demand consistently exceeds supply. Allocation is determined by Article 4 metric rankings — high performers receive priority assignment.

Operatives who have raised questions about the allocation policy are reminded that it exists to ensure the most effective use of limited resources. Operatives who have raised questions about what 'limited resources' means given that demand has exceeded supply for four consecutive cycles are reminded of Article 1.
